evidence_excerpt: Case 2: "The patient (presently one year old) is well, and biochemical and hematological testing show no signs of liver failure."

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Follow-up notes state "biochemical and hematological testing show no signs of liver failure," indicating absence of ongoing liver-related symptoms.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: "The patient’s clinical conditions began to progressively improve as of the first month of life."
At one year: "The patient ... is well," indicating sustained recovery in clinical status.

notes_on_uncertainty: The report includes two siblings with neonatal hemochromatosis; this extraction focuses on Case 2, who had documented follow-up to one year. Structural liver status at long term is uncertain because parents refused control liver biopsy, despite earlier biopsy showing cirrhosis. Timing is approximated relative to the documented diagnosis at 1 day of life.
